briefing.headline: Rig up a Deep Diving Crankbait — finesse winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Deep Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-pound bag.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on point, creek channel, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: 3XD for the deep zone in cold water. Smaller profile, tight action, gets 12-15ft on 10lb fluoro. Fish steeper rocky banks, deeper transition zones. Make long casts to keep the bait in the zone fo
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on point, creek channel, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: In clear deep lakes with shad kills, fish often suspend. The higher they are in the water column, the more active they are. Fish off main lake points, humps, drops, centers of big bays — not near the bank. Alabama rig with spinners (keeps rig...

Robertson: When following postspawn bass down ditches with a deep diver, start by casting ahead and up on top, then bring the crankbait down the side of the ditch at about a 45-degree angle. The crankbait must hit bottom.

Wheeler: On highland reservoirs like Cherokee, smallmouth live around holes/depressions (20 ft around the side) — if rock is around them the smallies live there. If a rock vein goes into the middle, they live on those veins. The holes harbor the b...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal winter is the toughest bite of the year. Fish retreat to the deepest structure available (20-35ft+) and barely move. Target steep structure where fish can access deep water without traveling — bluffs, vertical channel walls, bridge pilings.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
